scenario: Lumen Logistics tracks 11000 shipments per day on Camunda 8.8 and stores a pickupTimestamp as a date and time value such as date and time("2012-12-25T11:00:00") on the process instance. A modeller needs the documented FEEL call from the conversion functions page that extracts only the date component date("2012-12-25") from that date and time value on the process variable.
question: Which call on the conversion functions page extracts the date component from date and time("2012-12-25T11:00:00")?

options.text: to date(date and time("2012-12-25T11:00:00")); the docs document a to date() conversion function that ingests a date and time value and returns its date component as a FEEL date value on the conversion functions page

date(date and time("2012-12-25T11:00:00")); the docs document date(from: date and time): date as a signature of date() that extracts the date component from the given date and time value and show it returning date("2012-12-25")

date part(date and time("2012-12-25T11:00:00")); the docs document date part() as the FEEL conversion function that returns the date component from a date and time value on the conversion functions page
as date(date and time("2012-12-25T11:00:00")); the docs document as date() as the FEEL conversion function that returns a date from a date and time value on the conversion functions page

a.text: Incorrect. The conversion functions page does not document a to date() function; the function that extracts the date component from a date and time value is date(from: date and time).

b.text: Correct. The conversion functions page documents date(from: date and time): date as a signature of date() that extracts the date component from the given date and time, and shows date(date and time("2012-12-25T11:00:00")) returning date("2012-12-25").

c.text: Incorrect. The conversion functions page does not document a date part() function; the function that extracts the date component from a date and time value is date(from: date and time).

d.text: Incorrect. The conversion functions page does not document an as date() function; the function that returns a date from a date and time value is date(from: date and time).

explanation: The date() function has two signatures on the conversion functions page. date(from: string): date parses a calendar date string, and date(from: date and time): date extracts the date component from a date and time value. The page shows date(date and time("2012-12-25T11:00:00")) returning date("2012-12-25").
